bead.title: fix drain-queue golden selectors for the workers page
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The drain-queue golden tests are failing because the workers page now exposes more than one heading named `Workers`, and the Playwright spec is using a non-unique role query. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` calls `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Workers' })`, but the page also renders `Reported workers`, which makes the selector ambiguous in strict mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` uses a heading locator that collides with the page’s section heading.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:1-120` and its rendered output define the page structure that exposes both the main page heading and the workers subsection heading.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the drain-queue test target the unique page heading or otherwise disambiguate the query so the golden flow can verify the worker dispatch and terminal-state assertions. Keep the fix confined to the drain e2e spec and any minimal accessibility label adjustment required to make the heading unique.

NON-SCOPE
- Worker dispatch semantics.
- GraphQL or backend worker state transitions.
- Other e2e specs.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Both drain-queue golden tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` pass.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
